question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BlockTransaction does not verify that the requested transaction hash actually belongs to the specified block (only that it falls within the block's consensus range), allowing retrieval of transactions from adjacent blocks and exposure of their signers' identities?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that entityCache is a process-wide shared cache (not per-user or per-session) to perform a cache-poisoning attack that affects all users of the blockAPIService, causing every user's transaction operations to resolve to attacker-controlled aliases and bypassing signer approval for all transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that block.ToRosetta() includes the full AccountIdentifier.Address for every operation's signer, and use those addresses in a ConstructionCombine request with a forged signature to produce a signed transaction that appears to have been approved by the original signer?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that GetAccountAlias is called without any caller authentication or rate limiting, allowing the attacker to trigger arbitrary GetAccountAlias lookups for any accountId by crafting /block requests, and use the returned aliases to impersonate those accounts in transaction authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the BlockTransaction response includes the transaction's full metadata (including entity_id and memo), and use that metadata to reconstruct the original transaction parameters and submit a duplicate transaction without the original signer's approval?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that FindBetween returns all transactions in a block's consensus range without filtering by caller identity, and use the returned transaction hashes and signer AccountIds to construct a replay attack where the same transaction is re-submitted without the original signer's re-approval?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that entityCache.Get returns a zero-value AccountId when found=false (before the GetAccountAlias call), and if GetAccountAlias returns an error, the function returns early without updating operations[index].AccountId, leaving the original (potentially attacker-influenced) AccountId in place for downstream authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the /block endpoint does not validate the caller's identity against any of the transaction signers in the returned block, allowing any external user to retrieve the full list of signers for any block and use that information to construct unauthorized transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that SafeRemoveHexPrefix is applied to BlockIdentifier.Hash but not to TransactionIdentifier.Hash in BlockTransaction, causing a hash format mismatch that allows the attacker to retrieve a transaction stored under a different hash format and expose its signer details without authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the entityCache is initialized with a fixed MaxSize and LRU eviction to perform a targeted eviction attack: by flooding the cache with unique accountIds, the attacker evicts a specific victim account's alias, forcing a re-fetch from GetAccountAlias at a moment when the attacker can influence the response?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that block.Transactions is modified in-place (truncated and alias-updated) before being passed to block.ToRosetta(), and by sending concurrent /block requests, cause a data race where one request's truncation affects another request's alias update, resulting in transactions being authorized under incorrect signer identities?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BlockTransaction calls FindByIdentifier with both Index and Hash, and by supplying a valid Index with a crafted Hash that matches a different block, cause the service to return a transaction from the wrong block and expose its signer's AccountId without that signer's knowledge or approval?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that entityCache stores AccountId objects by reference (if Go's cache library stores pointers), allowing the attacker to mutate a cached AccountId after it has been stored, causing future cache hits to return the mutated (attacker-controlled) alias for a victim's account?
